briefs.title: Burnaby updates its fees and charges bylaw — costs for city services may change
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026, updating the fees the city charges for its services and permits. If your business pays any city fees — such as for permits, licences, or inspections — the amounts you owe may have changed.
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated fee schedule to confirm what your business now owes for any city permits or licences.

briefs.title: Burnaby updates its bylaw notice enforcement rules — fines and notices may change
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially passed Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026, updating the rules for how the city issues and enforces bylaw notices (official written warnings or fines for breaking local rules).
briefs.action_required: Check Burnaby's updated bylaw notice rules to see if any changes affect how fines are issued to your business.

briefs.title: Zoning amendment for 2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue passes first and second reading
briefs.summary: City council approved the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment for two properties at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. The amendment must still pass a third reading and adoption before it takes effect.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near Springer Avenue, monitor council meetings for the third reading and final adoption.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council advances zoning change at 5058 Imperial Street despite split vote
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council passed the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment (a change to local land-use rules) for 5058 Imperial Street, moving the proposal forward over objections from the Mayor and three councillors.
briefs.action_required: If your business or property is near 5058 Imperial Street, monitor future council readings and public hearings for this zoning change.

briefs.title: Burnaby parking meter and EV charging meter bylaw officially amended
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has given final approval to Bylaw 14805, which amends the rules governing parking meters and electric vehicle (EV) charging meters in the city.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business operates parking or EV charging meters in Burnaby and review the updated bylaw for any new requirements.
briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan Implementation Action Plan project now underway
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has received the project initiation for the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan — a roadmap that turns the city's long-term land use and development vision into concrete steps.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby's OCP process for upcoming public consultations or zoning changes that may affect your property or business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 6800 Lougheed Highway rezoning moves forward after first and second council reading
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council approved the first and second readings of a zoning bylaw amendment for the property at 6800 Lougheed Highway, moving the rezoning application one step closer to approval.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates at or near 6800 Lougheed Hwy, monitor council agendas for the public hearing date.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: City reviews 2025 investment results and sets 2026 financial forecast
briefs.summary: City council received a report on how its 2025 investment program performed and what financial returns are expected in 2026. No new rules or requirements for businesses were introduced.

briefs.title: 12-Storey, 290-Unit Mixed-Use Building Approved for 6535 Kingsway in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has directed staff to prepare a rezoning bylaw (a formal rule change that allows a different type of building on a property) for a 12-storey development at 6535 Kingsway, adding 290 homes and ground-floor commercial space.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near 6535 Kingsway, monitor the rezoning bylaw process for construction impacts and new commercial tenancy opportunities.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Five-Storey Self-Storage Facility Approved for 2757 Norland Avenue — Rezoning Bylaw Incoming
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has approved moving forward with a rezoning bylaw (a rule that changes how land can be used) for a five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue. No public hearing will be held because the project already fits the city's Official Community Plan.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council approves Poverty Reduction Strategy; implementation plan now in development
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has officially endorsed a Poverty Reduction Strategy and directed staff to build an implementation plan alongside community partners.

briefs.title: Carrigan Court mixed-use development rezoning application approved to move forward
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council has authorized staff to accept a rezoning application for 3856 & 3870 Carrigan Court, allowing a high-density mixed-use building with commercial, residential strata (privately owned units), and rental units to be developed.
briefs.action_required: If your business operates near Carrigan Court, monitor rezoning progress for potential impacts on your location.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: 3900 Grand Promenade rezoning swaps office space for hotel, adds 362 new strata units
briefs.summary: Council approved a rezoning change at 3900 Grand Promenade that removes planned commercial office space and replaces it with a hotel and restaurant. The project also grows by 362 strata units (privately owned condos), and affordable housing requirements are moved to a nearby site called Carrigan.
briefs.action_required: If your business plans involve this site, review the updated zoning to confirm permitted uses before signing leases or contracts.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

briefs.title: 19 contractors awarded hired equipment services contracts paid by actual quantities delivered
briefs.summary: Council approved contracts with 19 separate contractors to supply hired equipment services. Payments will be based on the actual amount of work or materials delivered, not a fixed price.

briefs.title: City awards $1.155M asphalt paving contract renewal to Blackrete Paving
briefs.summary: City council approved a contract renewal with Blackrete Paving Ltd. to provide asphalt paving services for the Engineering Department, valued at $1,155,000 including taxes.

briefs.title: $4.2M water metering contract awarded to Neptune Technology Group Canada
briefs.summary: A municipal council awarded a $4.2 million contract to Neptune Technology Group Canada Co. to supply and deliver water metering technology, taxes included.

briefs.title: Modular Four-Plex Pilot Contract Sent Back for Review Before Award
briefs.summary: City council paused the contract award for a modular four-plex housing pilot at 8315 14th Avenue, sending it back to staff to find ways to manage the project more efficiently.

briefs.title: BC municipality approves $36,250.57 in UBCM membership dues for 2026
briefs.summary: Council approved paying $36,250.57 (including taxes) in 2026 membership dues to the Union of BC Municipalities, the provincial association that represents local governments.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority approves 2026–2030 Strategic Plan, shaping city housing development
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council has approved the Burnaby Housing Authority's five-year strategic plan for 2026–2030, setting the direction for publicly supported housing development in the city over the next five years.
briefs.action_required: Monitor BHA project announcements for development opportunities, zoning changes, or housing requirements affecting your property or business.
